title: In Memory File System
hints: Think of the file system as a tree where each node is either a directory or a file. How do you efficiently navigate from the root to any path?
Use a hash map or nested dictionaries to represent the directory structure, where keys are entry names and values are child nodes or file contents.
Each directory node needs to track its children; files store content. Handle root as a special empty node, and ensure ls results are sorted alphabetically.
